title: BLACK AFFINITY GROUPS - 211info
text: BLACK AFFINITY GROUP BLACK AFFINITY GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Facilitates affinity groups in schools, workplaces, and community spaces. These groups provide a safe space for participants to talk about navigating work, school, and daily life in white-dominant environments. Eligibility: Individuals who identify as Black or Mixed with most programs for ages 10-17 who identify as Black and male (cisgender, transgender, and non-binary) Hours: Varies by event Email (971) 319-1194 Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 319-1194 Main phone Main phone (971) 319-1194 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website to see event calendar or for more information FEES: Varies sliding scale is available DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: City of Portland Services: Mentoring Programs * Cultural/Racial Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - GET READY! STAY READY! If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
